film.title: The Man Who Laughs
film.overview: When a proud noble refuses to kiss the hand of the despotic King James in 1690, he is cruelly executed and his son surgically disfigured.

dialogues.dialogue: Monster! Monster!  Monster! You should not leave the house!  Drop-in or I loose the wolf! Get out of here, you shameless!  Gwynplaine, Gwynplaine!  Gwynplaine, are you okay?
dialogues.cause_raw: being called a monster possible physical deformity or abnormality)

dialogues.dialogue: Fame. The success of the Man Who Laughs.  Only the interior is no longer enough.  Do not have to be ashamed of.  The most frequented places are those where at least highlight.  You decide, Gwymplaine.
In poverty I grew up. In the winter I ever shivered.  The hunger I have ever tasted.  Shame, I drank.  And both I vomit on you.  Just, Monsieur Clancharlie!
